question: Where is the Pressurizer (PZR) located in relation to the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV)?

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a critical barrier to fission product release and provides structural support for various reactor components, with materials and fabrication methods chosen to ensure long-term integrity in accordance with ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It details how actuation valves are exercised during cold shutdown conditions, outlines the required testing procedures, and specifies that a one-time in-situ test of the DHRS function will be performed on the first installed reactor module as part of the initial test program.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the core, steam generators, and pressurizer, and serves as a critical barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design life.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the main Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and the integral pressurizer (PZR). These components are essential for containing reactor coolant, supporting safety systems, and maintaining system integrity.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control systems.

edges.description: Section B specifies that the pressurizer is located in the upper region of the reactor vessel, which Section A describes as part of the NuScale Power Module, thus providing the specific spatial relationship between the pressurizer and the reactor pressure vessel.

Section A and Section B both state that the pressurizer is contained within the same reactor pressure vessel (RPV) as the reactor core and steam generators, directly connecting the physical location of the pressurizer to the RPV.

Section A states that the pressurizer (PZR) is contained within the same reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B describes operational procedures involving the PZR and its connection to the RPV, confirming their physical and functional proximity.

Section B describes the process of venting nitrogen from the Pressurizer (PZR) through the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV) high point degasification line, indicating a physical connection and relative location between the PZR and RPV, while Section A discusses high-point vents in the Reactor Coolant System, which are relevant to the venting process mentioned in Section B.

Section B states that the PZR volume is located at the top of the RPV, providing a direct spatial relationship, while Section A does not address PZR location, so Section B provides the relevant information for the analysis question.

Section A describes operational steps involving the PZR, including venting nitrogen through the RPV high point degasification line, while Section B specifies that the PZR volume is located at the top of the RPV and details the degasification line's function, thus Section B provides the physical and functional context for the actions described in Section A.
